comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text:
## Scope

Add pluggable compaction strategy interface to Tactus for memory management.

## Implementation

**New file**: `tactus/core/compaction_strategies.py`
- `CompactionStrategy` ABC with methods:
  - `should_compact(messages, estimated_tokens, max_tokens) -> bool`
  - `compact(messages, max_tokens) -> Tuple[List[Dict], Dict[str, Any]]`
- `RollingSummaryStrategy` implementation:
  - Constructor params: `llm_backend`, `recent_window`, `summary_prompt_template`
  - Compaction logic: preserve system messages + rolling summary + recent N messages
  - LLM-generated summary of middle messages
  - Returns compacted messages + metadata (summary, tokens_saved, etc)

**Constants**:
- `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PROMPT`: Generic template for conversation summarization

## Repository

This work happens in **Tactus** repository at `~/Projects/Tactus`

## Dependencies

None - foundational interface.

## Testing

Unit tests for:
- CompactionStrategy interface contract
- RollingSummaryStrategy.should_compact() logic
- Summary generation and insertion
- Token estimation accuracy
- Metadata correctness (counts, tokens_before/after)

comments.author: derek.norrbom
comments.text: Completed in Tactus repository as tcts-4bdc03. CompactionStrategy ABC and RollingSummaryStrategy implemented with LLM-based summarization.
